title: Evaluating public opinion data
description: Test your skills on evaluating conclusions in public opinion data.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best explains how a politician might use data found in a public opinion poll? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To craft the presentation of policy so as to gain public support
To anticipate how media organizations will report on policy
To encourage voters to support their policy position
To measure the effectiveness of public policies

assessment_items.question_content:
>“I believe great decisions are made with care, made with conviction, not made with polls. I do not need to take your pulse before I know my own mind. I do not reinvent myself at every turn. I am not running in borrowed clothes. When I act, you will know my reasons.” 
>-George W. Bush, Acceptance Speech, Republican National Convention, August 3, 2000

**Which of the following statements best summarizes Governor Bush’s argument?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: He does not use polls to decide his stance on policy
He uses polls to inform the media of his policy stances
He does not use polls as they are often unreliable
He uses polls to defend his policies against Congressional opposition

assessment_items.question_content:
>“We used polling not to determine what positions he would take but to figure out which of the positions he had already taken were the most popular. I would always draw the distinction between deciding on policy and identifying certain issues for emphasis by telling Clinton, ‘You print the menu of the things you want. Then I’ll advise which dish to have for dinner tonight.’”

>-Dick Morris, polling consultant for Bill Clinton, *Behind the Oval* , 1997

**Based on the text, which of the following statements would the author most likely agree with?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Public opinion poll results can be used to determine how best to present policies to the public
Public opinion poll results should be used to decide a politician’s stance on an issue
Public opinion polls results are often inaccurate as pollsters do not limit respondents to those who are informed on policy issues
Public opinion poll results can be used to predict who will win an election to a high degree of accuracy

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/861884bb2e498d046deb08468f26e36e.png)

>Source: [Quinnipiac polls](https://poll.qu.edu/national/release-detail?ReleaseID=2525)

**Which of the following statements best describes a trend in the line graph above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Support for gun control legislation has consistently hovered around 60% in the period shown
Support for gun control legislation generally increased in this period
Opposition to gun control legislation generally increased in the period shown.
Opposition to gun control legislation decreased substantially from 2017 to 2018

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/861884bb2e498d046deb08468f26e36e.png)

>Source: [Quinnipiac polls](https://poll.qu.edu/national/release-detail?ReleaseID=2525)

After reading the results of this public opinion poll, Congressman Dan Schubert proposes a bill that would eliminate mandatory background checks on individuals purchasing guns.

**Which of the following is an accurate conclusion based on a comparison of the trends in the graph and your knowledge of the importance of public opinion data as a source of political influence?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Congressman Schubert judged that although a majority of Americans support stricter gun control laws, the voters in his district are more likely to oppose such laws
Congressman Schubert judged that the poll’s methods were unscientific because it did not ask open-ended questions
Congressman Schubert believes that the support for stricter gun control measures among Americans is short-lived and likely to dip below 50% in the near future
Congressman Schubert believes in acting as a delegate for his constituents, not a trustee, and therefore votes his conscience rather than responding to public opinion

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/52c4fbedcbb2b33f17a2aa6bb56c1eac.png)

**Which of the following is an accurate conclusion based on a comparison of the trends in the line graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Republicans placed more trust in the information from national news media in 2017 than in 2016
Fewer national news organizations represent the Republican Party than the Democratic Party
Democrats are more engaged with national media coverage than are Republicans
Trust in information from national news media is increasingly related to partisan affiliation

assessment_items.question_content:
Two market research firms conduct public opinion polls in the month before the city council votes on a proposed ordinance to issue fines to homeless individuals. Each firm uses a random-dialing system to contact 1000 city residents. 

DynamicData’s survey asked: “Do you think homeless people should be issued a fine for making our city streets unsanitary?” 53% of respondents answered yes, 41% answered no, and 6% said they didn’t know. 

Reardon Research’s survey asked: “Do you think homeless veterans should be punished for sleeping in the streets?” 23% of respondents answered yes, 68% of respondents answered no, and 10% said they didn’t know. 

**Which of the following most likely accounts for the different results?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The questions contained a difference in wording
Neither poll had a large enough sample size to produce scientific results
The polls were conducted at different times
The polls relied on random-dialing, which rarely produces a representative sample

assessment_items.question_content:
In a public opinion poll, 2 million subscribers to *Soccer Lovers Magazine* were called and asked to agree, disagree, or express neutrality toward the following statement: **FIFA, soccer’s international governing organization, should mandate that soccer clubs pay female athletes the same amount as male athletes.** 

The magazine reported a sampling error of ±3% and published their findings in the following chart.

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/be397b29675fb322129eecf7221061b2.png)

**A pollster might claim that this poll is unreliable for which of the following reasons? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The poll was conducted solely among respondents who purchased a magazine and does not rely on a randomized sample
The poll did not use open-ended statements, creating a poll that leads respondents to an opinion
The poll reported a large sampling error, indicating that the margin of error is high
The poll’s sample size was too small to be considered a true representative sample

assessment_items.question_content:
An animal rights organization conducts a random digit dialing survey, asking 1,500 respondents to answer the following question: **“Should the United States repeal a law that requires responsible dog owners to pay for expensive and invasive surgery on a dog to prevent them from fathering cute puppies?”**

The organization reported a sampling error of ±4% and published its findings in the following chart. 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/42f5c51eac7afec46987de8a318b000a.png)

**A pollster might claim that this poll is unreliable for which of the following reasons? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The poll used a biased question that strongly influenced respondents to have one opinion
The poll did not report a sampling error, refusing to show the margin of error
The poll’s sample size was too small to be considered a true representative sample
The poll was conducted by random digit dialing system, making it hard to track the demographics of respondents

assessment_items.question_content:
A fitness website, Pump it up!, conducts a survey outside several gyms in Seattle, Washington. They interviewed a total of 100 people and asked them to respond to the following question: **“How many days a week should a person work out to be considered healthy?”**

The website reported a sampling error of ±3% and published their findings in the following chart. 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ea498b45efdcee1ca544d568d8dada4b.png)

**A pollster might claim that this poll is unreliable for which of the following reasons? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The poll’s sample respondents were not randomized and not representative of the average Seattle resident
The poll reported a small sampling error, indicating that they did not measure the margin of error
The poll used open-ended questions, making it difficult to track respondents’ answers
The poll was conducted within the city limits and therefore could not come to a reliable conclusion

assessment_items.question_content:
A major textbook distributor, Thomas textbooks, conducts a telephone survey of teachers that use one of their textbooks. They interviewed a total of 2000 teachers (out of 10,000 teachers who used a textbook) and asked them to agree, disagree, or express neutrality toward the following statement: “My students’ standardized test scores improved after using a Thomas textbook.”

The textbook company reported a sampling error of ±25% and published their findings in the following chart. 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/da1829d1779dfac105e2c09d694a063c.png)

**A pollster might claim that this poll is unreliable for which of the following reasons? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The poll reports a large sampling error, indicating that the margin of error is high
The poll did not use open-ended statements, creating a poll that leads respondents to an opinion
The poll’s sample size is too small to be considered a true representative sample
The poll was conducted over the telephone not in person, getting questionable responses

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/52c4fbedcbb2b33f17a2aa6bb56c1eac.png)

**Which of the following accurately describes a limitation of the visual representation of the data? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It uses percentages instead of raw numbers, which distorts the comparison
The data would be much clearer displayed using a pie graph instead of a line graph
The data in the graph is unreliable based on how it was collected and the bias of the organization conducting the survey
Predicting trends or making generalizations based on two data points is unreliable
